query: What are the steps to sign up for a course or an examination?

queries: What are the steps to sign up for a course or an examination?

retrieved.title: Registration for courses and examinations
retrieved.text:
The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).
- If you have any questions about the registration (registration periods, limited numbers of study places), please contact your [studies service center (SSC)/studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).
- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

[### Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-non-continuous-assessment/)

[### Registration for courses with continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-continuous-assessment/)

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment
retrieved.text: ## / - Step 1: Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner You can find the record of examinations in u:space under the tab “Studies”. It is organised according to the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which courses you have already completed, which achievements you have to complete next and what requirements you have to fulfil for them. You can find the semester planner in u:space under the tab “Courses”. It shows you the courses currently offered and provides you with a calendar to help you plan your semester. Attention: Please always check the [curriculum of your degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula). The record of examinations in u:space supports you in planning, but you have to select the correct curriculum point yourself on the basis of your curriculum when registering for the course or exam. - Step 2: Select an achievement from your curriculum Search for modules for which you still have to complete courses (red and orange traffic light symbol) in your record of examinations in u:space. After that with the help of the record of examinations and by looking at your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula), determine for which curriculum point (module) you are still missing a course / exam. To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment
retrieved.text: Please select the course and the curriculum point for which this course is to be credited and click on "Register". This completes the registration for the selected course. The green color, the checkmark and the status "Registered" show you that the registration was successful. If you want to cancel your registration for the course, click on the button "Deregister".

retrieved.title: Registration for courses and examinations
retrieved.text: / - Step 1: Search examination on u:find You can see the details about the exam (e.g. date, time, venue, examiner(s), alternative exam dates) on the Course Directory [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Click on “Register/Deregister for this exam”. You will be forwarded to u:space, where you can complete your registration. Please click on the following link if you need [help in using u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html). - Step 2: Register for the examination You can only register for exams within the relevant registration period. You can check the registration period on u:find. In u:space you have to select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. Otherwise your grade will be assigned to the wrong module in the record of examinations). Click on “Register” to complete the registration. Now, your registration has the [registration status](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741418) “Registered"(Angemeldet) in u:space. If you do not meet all the necessary requirements (e.g. examinations from the introductory and orientation period) for taking the exam, your registration status will be "Prerequisite currently not met". In the details you will find the missing requirements. In this case, you are not yet able to register for the exam. If you meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline, your registration will be considered in the allocation process. For exams with

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: study places. [Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will offer three additional examination dates. The fourth examination attempt has to take place before an examination committee. Courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, practical courses) do not end with a final examination, but rather require you to complete two or more oral or written partial achievements. These partial achievements are the basis for determining your final grade. You can find your grade in the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass).

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text:
- [Allocation of places in courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c721851)
- [Compulsory attendance](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741378)
- [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741418)
- [Point allocation system](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741408)
- [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741408)
- [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741429)

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

#

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: and exams via u:find resp. the record of examinations in u:space. In order to be able to attend courses with continuous assessment or exams, you have to register in due time. You can find the registration periods within the respective courses in u:find. In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. You cannot be registered after the registration/late registration period has ended. If you have registered correctly, you are entitled to attend the course/exam. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration status in u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30191&cHash=71de51db4a7852d1f6b8c1de1a4de491) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - [Attempts](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31239&cHash=e87c5fe84f21a0875fa06b7e65176b98) - [Unauthorised absence from a course with continuous assessment/exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31313&cHash=c248aea8f5dc38f00ab9fb6e422e59cd) - Registration status in u:space In u:space, in the section > Studies > Registration Overview you can view the status of your registrations. For courses with non-continuous assessment (e.g. lectures) the following status values may appear: - Registered (Angemeldet): You have registered for the course. - Deregistered (Abgemeldet): You have successfully deregistered from the course. For courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars,

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in your degree programme, you can view the details of the course by clicking on the green list icon "More Details". - If you log in with your u:account, you can compile your personal "[watchlist](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html#merkliste)" by clicking on the star next to the course title. - For each course with existing dates, you can export the dates to your own calendar via an iCal link in the detailed view of the course or subscribe to an Internet calendar via the link. - Step 4: Register for courses and examinations As a general rule: You have to register for courses and examinations. Registration is possible via the course directory [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). You can only register for courses and examinations within certain registration periods (for courses with continuous assessment, these are usually in September and February). Places for courses/examinations are allocated after this period has ended. Certain courses may have a limited number of study places. [Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text: a pi LV After Step 3: “Select course in u:find” you will be forwarded to the registration system in u:space. Please select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. Otherwise your grade will be assigned to the wrong module in the record of examinations). For courses with a limited number of study places, you will have to assign points or preferences. After you assigned the points or ranked the groups you can click on “Register” to complete the registration. For courses with limited space - that are most of the courses with continuous assessment (pi LV) - you must assign points or preferences for the course during the registration process. For some pi LV groups are offered. **Groups** are parallel offers of a course, which can differ in name, place and time. When registering, you can select several groups and then prioritize them. The more groups you have selected, the more likely you are to get a place. As soon as the registration period has ended, the system will allocate the places on the courses. Registrations are organised and places will be allocated according to the specifications of the curricula (e.g. [points system](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c226340), [preference system](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c226332) or [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/)). Next, the [status of your registration](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c298840) will

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. The bachelor programme in [Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/) (as well as the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education) require the successful participation in the sporting aptitude admission test (only available in German). The test takes place twice per academic year (ahead of the respective winter and summer semester) and is valid for four semesters. Admission to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education also requires the successful participation in the entrance exam test for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. Choose your desired degree programme and follow the [step-by-step admission guide](/en/admission-procedure/).

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: The registration period for the course I would like to complete ends earlier than the application period for co-registration. What deadline do I have to meet? You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet

retrieved.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: Example: Adapted entrance exam procedure in a smaller testing venue
retrieved.text:
- ### Step 1

  **Before the written test**

  No later than 14 days before the test date, we will send you the admission confirmation and the access plan for the test rooms. In this e-mail, we will also inform you which items are permitted during the test.
- ### Step 2

  **Your admission on the test day**

  You will find your time slot and access point on your admission confirmation. We will meet you there, verify your identity, and then accompany you to your test room.
- ### Step 3

  **The written test**

  In the test room, you will have a few minutes to settle in. The test administrators will then read out the instructions, including all the details regarding the examination process

  At least one supervisor will be in the room with you throughout the duration of the exam.
- ### Step 4

  **After the written test**

  Once the test time has expired, we collect the documents and check that they are complete. We will escort you back to the exit.

  After the test has been evaluated, you will receive an e-mail about the next steps in the admission procedure.

retrieved.title: The day of the written test

retrieved.text:

[Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will offer three additional examination dates. The fourth examination attempt has to take place before an examination committee. Courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars, practical courses) do not end with a final examination, but rather require you to complete two or more oral or written partial achievements. These partial achievements are the basis for determining your final grade. You can find your grade in the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass).

reranked.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
reranked.text:
- [Allocation of places in courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c721851)
- [Compulsory attendance](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741378)
- [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741418)
- [Point allocation system](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741408)
- [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741408)
- [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741429)

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

#

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: and exams via u:find resp. the record of examinations in u:space. In order to be able to attend courses with continuous assessment or exams, you have to register in due time. You can find the registration periods within the respective courses in u:find. In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. You cannot be registered after the registration/late registration period has ended. If you have registered correctly, you are entitled to attend the course/exam. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration status in u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30191&cHash=71de51db4a7852d1f6b8c1de1a4de491) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - [Attempts](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31239&cHash=e87c5fe84f21a0875fa06b7e65176b98) - [Unauthorised absence from a course with continuous assessment/exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31313&cHash=c248aea8f5dc38f00ab9fb6e422e59cd) - Registration status in u:space In u:space, in the section > Studies > Registration Overview you can view the status of your registrations. For courses with non-continuous assessment (e.g. lectures) the following status values may appear: - Registered (Angemeldet): You have registered for the course. - Deregistered (Abgemeldet): You have successfully deregistered from the course. For courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars,

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in your degree programme, you can view the details of the course by clicking on the green list icon "More Details". - If you log in with your u:account, you can compile your personal "[watchlist](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html#merkliste)" by clicking on the star next to the course title. - For each course with existing dates, you can export the dates to your own calendar via an iCal link in the detailed view of the course or subscribe to an Internet calendar via the link. - Step 4: Register for courses and examinations As a general rule: You have to register for courses and examinations. Registration is possible via the course directory [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). You can only register for courses and examinations within certain registration periods (for courses with continuous assessment, these are usually in September and February). Places for courses/examinations are allocated after this period has ended. Certain courses may have a limited number of study places. [Information about the registration system and the registration deadlines](/en/semester-planning/registration-for-coursesexaminations/) - Step 5: Complete courses The first examination date for a course with non-continuous assessment will take place after the last unit of the semester. Subsequently, the university will

reranked.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
reranked.text: a pi LV After Step 3: “Select course in u:find” you will be forwarded to the registration system in u:space. Please select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. Otherwise your grade will be assigned to the wrong module in the record of examinations). For courses with a limited number of study places, you will have to assign points or preferences. After you assigned the points or ranked the groups you can click on “Register” to complete the registration. For courses with limited space - that are most of the courses with continuous assessment (pi LV) - you must assign points or preferences for the course during the registration process. For some pi LV groups are offered. **Groups** are parallel offers of a course, which can differ in name, place and time. When registering, you can select several groups and then prioritize them. The more groups you have selected, the more likely you are to get a place. As soon as the registration period has ended, the system will allocate the places on the courses. Registrations are organised and places will be allocated according to the specifications of the curricula (e.g. [points system](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c226340), [preference system](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c226332) or [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/)). Next, the [status of your registration](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c298840) will

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. The bachelor programme in [Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/) (as well as the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education) require the successful participation in the sporting aptitude admission test (only available in German). The test takes place twice per academic year (ahead of the respective winter and summer semester) and is valid for four semesters. Admission to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education also requires the successful participation in the entrance exam test for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. Choose your desired degree programme and follow the [step-by-step admission guide](/en/admission-procedure/).

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: The registration period for the course I would like to complete ends earlier than the application period for co-registration. What deadline do I have to meet? You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet

reranked.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: Example: Adapted entrance exam procedure in a smaller testing venue
reranked.text:
- ### Step 1

  **Before the written test**

  No later than 14 days before the test date, we will send you the admission confirmation and the access plan for the test rooms. In this e-mail, we will also inform you which items are permitted during the test.
- ### Step 2

  **Your admission on the test day**

  You will find your time slot and access point on your admission confirmation. We will meet you there, verify your identity, and then accompany you to your test room.
- ### Step 3

  **The written test**

  In the test room, you will have a few minutes to settle in. The test administrators will then read out the instructions, including all the details regarding the examination process

  At least one supervisor will be in the room with you throughout the duration of the exam.
- ### Step 4

  **After the written test**

  Once the test time has expired, we collect the documents and check that they are complete. We will escort you back to the exit.

  After the test has been evaluated, you will receive an e-mail about the next steps in the admission procedure.

reranked.title: The day of the written test

selected.title: Registration for courses and examinations
selected.text:
The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).
- If you have any questions about the registration (registration periods, limited numbers of study places), please contact your [studies service center (SSC)/studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).
- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

[### Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-non-continuous-assessment/)

[### Registration for courses with continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-continuous-assessment/)

selected.title: Registration for courses and examinations

Please click on the following link if you need [help in using u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html). - Step 2: Register for the examination You can only register for exams within the relevant registration period. You can check the registration period on u:find. In u:space you have to select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. Otherwise your grade will be assigned to the wrong module in the record of examinations). Click on “Register” to complete the registration. Now, your registration has the [registration status](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741418) “Registered"(Angemeldet) in u:space. If you do not meet all the necessary requirements (e.g. examinations from the introductory and orientation period) for taking the exam, your registration status will be "Prerequisite currently not met". In the details you will find the missing requirements. In this case, you are not yet able to register for the exam. Please click on the following link if you need [help in using u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html). - Step 2: Register for the examination You can only register for exams within the relevant registration period. You can check the registration period on u:find. In u:space you have to select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. Otherwise your grade will be assigned to the wrong module in the record of examinations). Click on “Register” to complete the registration. Now, your registration has the [registration status](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741418) “Registered"(Angemeldet) in u:space. If you do not meet all the necessary requirements (e.g. examinations from the introductory and orientation period) for taking the exam, your registration status will be "Prerequisite currently not met". In the details you will find the missing requirements. In this case, you are not yet able to register for the exam. If you meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline, your registration will be considered in the allocation process. For exams with

[6] Registration for courses and examinations — Registration for courses
/ - Step 1: Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner You can find the record of examinations in u:space under the tab "Studies". It is organised according to the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which courses you have already completed, which achievements you have to complete next and what requirements you have to fulfil for them. You can find the semester planner in u:space under the tab "Courses". It shows you the courses currently offered and provides you with a calendar to help you plan your semester. Attention: Please always check the [curriculum of your degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula). The record of examinations in u:space supports you in planning, but you have to select the correct study plan point yourself on the basis of your curriculum when registering for the course or exam. - Step 2: Select an achievement from your curriculum Search for modules for which you still have to complete courses (red and orange traffic light symbol) in your record of examinations in u:space. After that with the help of the record of examinations and by looking at your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula), determine for which curriculum point (module) you are still missing a course / exam. To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon

4.title: Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Registration for courses and examinations
The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).
- If you have any questions about the registration (registration periods, limited numbers of study places), please contact your [studies service center (SSC)/studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).
- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

[### Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-non-continuous-assessment/)

[### Registration for courses with continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-continuous-assessment/)

[2] Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment — Registration steps
## / - Step 1: Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner You can find the record of examinations in u:space under the tab “Studies”. It is organised according to the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which courses you have already completed, which achievements you have to complete next and what requirements you have to fulfil for them. You can find the semester planner in u:space under the tab “Courses”. It shows you the courses currently offered and provides you with a calendar to help you plan your semester. Attention: Please always check the [curriculum of your degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula). The record of examinations in u:space supports you in planning, but you have to select the correct curriculum point yourself on the basis of your curriculum when registering for the course or exam. - Step 2: Select an achievement from your curriculum Search for modules for which you still have to complete courses (red and orange traffic light symbol) in your record of examinations in u:space. After that with the help of the record of examinations and by looking at your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula), determine for which curriculum point (module) you are still missing a course / exam. To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon

[4] Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment — Registration steps
Please select the course and the curriculum point for which this course is to be credited and click on "Register". This completes the registration for the selected course. The green color, the checkmark and the status "Registered" show you that the registration was successful. If you want to cancel your registration for the course, click on the button "Deregister".

[5] Registration for courses and examinations — Registration for exams
/ - Step 1: Search examination on u:find You can see the details about the exam (e.g. date, time, venue, examiner(s), alternative exam dates) on the Course Directory [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Click on “Register/Deregister for this exam”. You will be forwarded to u:space, where you can complete your registration. Please click on the following link if you need [help in using u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/help.html). - Step 2: Register for the examination You can only register for exams within the relevant registration period. You can check the registration period on u:find. In u:space you have to select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the correct one as it is also listed in the Course Directory u:find. In the details you will find the missing requirements. In this case, you are not yet able to register for the exam. If you meet the requirements by the end of the registration deadline, your registration will be considered in the allocation process. For exams with

Question: What are the steps to sign up for a course or an examination?

generate.raw_response:
To sign up for a course, the steps are: 
1. Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner in u:space [2][3][6], 
2. Select an achievement from your curriculum [2][3][6]. 
To sign up for an examination, the steps are: 
1. Search for the examination on u:find [5], 
2. Register for the examination in u:space [5].

answer:
To sign up for a course, the steps are: 
1. Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner in u:space [2][3][6], 
2. Select an achievement from your curriculum [2][3][6]. 
To sign up for an examination, the steps are: 
1. Search for the examination on u:find [5], 
2. Register for the examination in u:space [5].
